# DDI-Compliant SSOT Schema (`ddi-metadata.yaml`)

This is the **Single Source of Truth** shared by all skills in the survey-cleaning harness.
Every skill reads from and writes to this schema. The structure maps directly to DDI Lifecycle 3.3
and DDI-CDI 1.0 concepts. Do not invent fields — use only what is defined here.

---

## Full Schema

```yaml
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ddi-metadata.yaml
# DDI Lifecycle 3.3 compliant survey dataset metadata
# Generated by codebook-parse. Edited by researcher. Consumed by all skills.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

schema_version: "1.0"          # This schema version (not the DDI version)
ddi_version: "3.3"             # DDI Lifecycle version used as reference

# ── Study-level (DDI: StudyUnit) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
study:
  name: "CGSS 2021"
  id: null                     # DDI URN or local identifier
  agency: null                 # Producing agency
  universe: null               # Plain-language universe statement → DDI Universe
  analysis_unit: Person        # DDI AnalysisUnit CV: Person | Household | Organization | Other
  data_source: null            # Original file path or URL
  wave: null                   # Wave identifier for panel/repeated cross-section

# ── Shared category schemes (DDI: CategoryScheme) ────────────────────────────
# Define once, reference by name in representation.category_scheme_ref.
# Use for Likert scales or code lists shared across many variables.
shared_category_schemes:
  # Example:
  # agree5:
  #   1: "Strongly disagree"
  #   2: "Disagree"
  #   3: "Neither agree nor disagree"
  #   4: "Agree"
  #   5: "Strongly agree"

# ── Shared missing value schemas (DDI: ManagedMissingValuesRepresentation) ───
# Define once, reference by name in variable.missing.schema_ref
shared_missing_schemas:
  # Example — define one or more named schemas for reuse across variables
  # standard:
  #   codes:
  #     -9: { label: "Refused",       type: refused }
  #     -8: { label: "Don't know",    type: dont_know }
  #     -7: { label: "Not applicable",type: inapplicable }
  #     -3: { label: "Missing",       type: missing_data }
  #   ranges: []
  #   blank_is_missing: true

# ── Variables (DDI: VariableScheme > Variable > RepresentedVariable) ─────────
variables:
  - id: var001                 # Internal stable identifier (do not change after creation)
    name: q1_gender            # Variable name in dataset
    label: "Respondent gender" # Human-readable label → DDI Variable.Label

    # DDI Variable classification flags
    is_temporal: false         # DDI Variable.isGeographic / temporal flag
    is_geographic: false       # DDI Variable.isGeographic
    is_weight: false           # DDI Variable.isWeight

    # DDI ConceptualVariable: Concept + UnitType
    concept: Gender            # Concept being measured (free text or CV term)
    unit_type: Person          # Who/what is being measured → DDI Universe

    # Per-variable universe (DDI: Universe, narrower than study.universe)
    # Describes the subpopulation this specific variable was collected for.
    # Inferred from questionnaire skip logic + variable labels.
    # Example: "employed respondents aged 18–65" (from "if employed, ask Q42")
    # Leave null when the variable applies to the full study universe.
    universe: null             # str|null — AI-inferred per-variable universe

    # DDI RepresentedVariable: how values are stored
    representation:
      # type: code | numeric | scale | text | datetime | geographic
      type: code

      # DDI RecommendedDataType — controls R casting in pipeline-generate
      # integer | float | string | boolean
      storage_type: integer

      # For type: code → DDI CodeRepresentation + CategoryScheme
      # Option A: inline codes (for variable-specific categories)
      codes:
        1: "Male"
        2: "Female"
      # Option B: reference a shared category scheme (set codes: {} when using this)
      category_scheme_ref: null   # Name of a shared_category_schemes entry, or null

      classification_level: nominal   # nominal | ordinal | interval | ratio
                                      # Only valid for type: code

      # For type: numeric → DDI NumericRepresentation
      # min: 0
      # max: 9997
      # topcode: 9997             # Values at or above this imply "X or more"
      # decimal_positions: 0
      # unit: null                # Measurement unit (e.g. CNY, years, kg)

      # For type: scale → DDI ScaleRepresentation
      # min: 1
      # max: 5
      # anchors:                  # Labelled endpoints
      #   1: "Strongly disagree"
      #   5: "Strongly agree"
      # Note: classification_level is NOT valid for type: scale (DDI constraint)

      # For type: text → DDI TextRepresentation
      # min_length: null
      # max_length: null
      # pattern: null             # Regex

      # For type: datetime → DDI DateTimeRepresentation
      # format: null              # e.g. "YYYY-MM"

    # DDI ManagedMissingValuesRepresentation (per-variable, or by reference)
    missing:
      schema_ref: null          # Name of a shared_missing_schemas entry, or null
      codes:                    # Per-variable overrides / additions (MissingCodeRepresentation)
        {}
        # -9: { label: "Refused",  type: refused }
      ranges:                   # Range-based missing codes (MissingNumericRepresentation)
        []
        # - { low: 88, high: 99, label: "System missing", type: missing_data }
      blank_is_missing: true    # DDI ManagedMissingValuesRepresentation.IsBlankMissingValue

    # DDI Variable.SourceVariableReference (only for derived variables)
    source_variables: []        # List of variable `id` values this was derived from
    derivation_rule: null       # Plain-language or R expression describing the transformation

    # Flags set during parsing (researcher should review)
    _parse_flags: []            # e.g. ["missing_codes_inferred", "label_truncated"]
    _needs_review: false        # Set true when AI confidence in any field is below threshold.
                                # Researcher must verify this variable before proceeding.

# ── Data structure (DDI-CDI: DataStructure) ──────────────────────────────────
data_structure:
  type: wide                   # wide | long | panel | multidimensional
  id_vars: []                  # Uniquely identifying variable names
  wave_var: null               # Variable name encoding wave/year (panel/repeated)
  strata_var: null             # Sampling stratum variable
  cluster_var: null            # Primary sampling unit variable
  weight_var: null             # Survey weight variable

# ── Processing history (DDI: ProcessingEvent) ────────────────────────────────
# Append-only. Each skill adds its own entry.
processing_events:
  - event_id: evt001
    # DDI ProcessingEvent type CV:
    # CleaningOperation | ControlOperation | DataAppraisal | WeightingOperation
    type: CleaningOperation
    timestamp: null            # ISO 8601
    skill_version: null
    description: "Initial metadata extraction from source codebook"
    inputs: []                 # Source file(s)
    outputs: ["ddi-metadata.yaml"]
    operator: null             # Human or agent that ran this
```

---

## DDI Concept Mapping

| SSOT field | DDI Lifecycle 3.3 element |
|---|---|
| `study` | `StudyUnit` |
| `study.universe` | `Universe` |
| `study.analysis_unit` | `AnalysisUnit` (CV) |
| `shared_category_schemes` | `CategoryScheme` (reusable) |
| `shared_missing_schemas` | `ManagedMissingValuesRepresentation` |
| `variables[].is_temporal` | `Variable.isGeographic` (temporal variant) |
| `variables[].is_geographic` | `Variable.isGeographic` |
| `variables[].is_weight` | `Variable.isWeight` |
| `variables[].concept` | `ConceptualVariable.Concept` |
| `variables[].unit_type` | `ConceptualVariable.UnitType` |
| `variables[].universe` | `Universe` (per-variable, narrower than `study.universe`) |
| `variables[].representation` | `RepresentedVariable.ValueRepresentation` |
| `variables[].representation.storage_type` | `RepresentedVariable.RecommendedDataType` |
| `variables[].representation.codes` | `CategoryScheme` + `CodeList` (inline) |
| `variables[].representation.category_scheme_ref` | `CategoryScheme` (by reference) |
| `variables[].representation.classification_level` | `CodeRepresentationBaseType.classificationLevel` |
| `variables[].missing.codes` | `MissingCodeRepresentation` |
| `variables[].missing.ranges` | `MissingNumericRepresentation` |
| `variables[].missing.blank_is_missing` | `IsBlankMissingValue` |
| `variables[].source_variables` | `Variable.SourceVariableReference` |
| `data_structure` | DDI-CDI `DataStructure` |
| `processing_events` | `ProcessingEvent` (CleaningOperation) |

## Missing Value Types

Use only these controlled vocabulary values for `missing.codes[].type` and `missing.ranges[].type`:

| type | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `refused` | Respondent refused to answer |
| `dont_know` | Respondent did not know |
| `inapplicable` | Question not applicable (skip logic) |
| `missing_data` | Data lost or not collected |
| `other_missing` | Other/unspecified missingness |

## Representation Classification Levels

Only valid when `representation.type: code`. Do not set for `scale`, `numeric`, or `text`.

| level | Use when |
|---|---|
| `nominal` | Unordered categories (gender, region) |
| `ordinal` | Ordered categories (education, Likert) |
| `interval` | Equal intervals, arbitrary zero (temperature) |
| `ratio` | Equal intervals, true zero (income, age) |

## Storage Types

`representation.storage_type` controls R casting in `pipeline-generate`. Maps to DDI `RecommendedDataType`:

| storage_type | R cast | Use when |
|---|---|---|
| `integer` | `as.integer()` | Categorical codes, count variables |
| `float` | `as.numeric()` | Continuous measurements, rates |
| `string` | `as.character()` | Free text, ID strings |
| `boolean` | `as.logical()` | Binary true/false flags |

## Processing Event Types

`processing_events[].type` must be one of the DDI controlled vocabulary values:

| type | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `CleaningOperation` | Recoding, relabelling, missing value treatment |
| `ControlOperation` | Consistency checks, range validation |
| `DataAppraisal` | Quality assessment, outlier flagging |
| `WeightingOperation` | Weight construction or application |

## Parse Flags

These are set automatically by `codebook-parse` to alert the researcher:

| flag | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `missing_codes_inferred` | Missing codes guessed from value range; verify |
| `label_truncated` | Original label exceeded 255 chars |
| `type_uncertain` | Could not determine numeric vs ordinal; review |
| `codes_incomplete` | Not all observed values have labels |
| `no_concept` | ConceptualVariable not mappable; fill manually |

## Review Flags

`_needs_review` is a boolean set by `codebook-parse` when the AI confidence in one or more fields falls below threshold. Unlike `_parse_flags` (which categorizes *what* is uncertain), `_needs_review: true` signals *that the researcher must verify this variable before proceeding*. Common triggers:

- `universe` inferred from skip logic with low confidence (e.g. ambiguous branching)
- `concept` mapping is a best-guess match
- Multiple `_parse_flags` on the same variable (compound uncertainty)
